name: Children
description: Products or content directed at minors (toys, children's media, educational products for kids). This is about the product category, not the audience — a financial services ad targeting parents is not 'children'.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US COPPA
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Children's Online Privacy Protection Act. Restricts data collection and targeting for children under 13. Parental consent required.

regulatory_frameworks.name: UK Age Appropriate Design Code (AADC)
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Applies to services likely to be accessed by children under 18. Requires age-appropriate design and limits on data use for targeting.

regulatory_frameworks.name: EU GDPR Article 8
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Parental consent required for children under 16 (member states may lower to 13). Most restrictive applicable threshold applies.

guidance: The most restrictive applicable age threshold applies. Certain targeting categories are entirely prohibited, not just size-constrained. Governance agents should treat this vertical as requiring the strictest audience protections across all jurisdictions in the campaign.
